data.title: Optery 2026: New Features That Actually Protect You
data.excerpt: Discover Optery's latest features that remove your data from brokers automatically. Try it free and see what's exposed about you today.

$media.alt: Optery 2026: New Features That Actually Protect You

children.text: I've spent years testing privacy tools. Most promise the moon and deliver a flashlight. Optery is different. It actually does what it claims—removes your personal information from data brokers without you lifting a finger. And the 2026 updates make it even better.
Let me walk you through what's new, why it matters, and whether it's right for you.
Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through my links, I may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. I only recommend tools I genuinely use and trust.
What Makes Optery Stand Out

Data brokers collect everything about you. Your name, address, phone number, email, family connections, even your purchasing habits. They package this information and sell it to anyone willing to pay. The result? Spam calls, targeted scams, and real security risks.
Optery tackles this head-on. It scans hundreds of data broker sites, finds your information, and submits removal requests on your behalf. Then it monitors to make sure your data stays gone.
What impressed me from day one was the transparency. You get a personalized exposure report showing exactly where your data appears. Screenshots included. No vague promises—actual evidence.
If you want the full breakdown of how the service works, I wrote a detailed
guide on what Optery is
that covers the mechanics in depth.
But here's the thing: Optery isn't resting on its laurels. The 2026 updates address real user feedback and add features that competitors still haven't figured out.
New Features Worth Your Attention
Optery rolled out several improvements this year. Each one solves a specific pain point I've heard from readers and experienced myself.
Expanded Broker Coverage

The platform now covers more data brokers than ever. We're talking hundreds of sites. When I first tested Optery, it scanned around 200 brokers. The 2026 version pushes that number significantly higher. More coverage means fewer gaps where your data can hide.
This matters because data brokers multiply. New ones pop up constantly. Optery keeps its database updated so you don't have to track them yourself.
Enhanced Exposure Reports
The exposure report was always a standout feature. Now it's even better. You get more detailed screenshots, clearer categorization, and better tracking of removal progress.

When I ran my latest scan, I discovered my old address was still listed on sites I'd never heard of. The report showed me exactly where, with timestamps and status updates. That visibility gives you real control.
If you're curious about
using Optery step by step
, I've put together a walkthrough that covers the setup process.
Google and Bing Search Integration

This one caught my attention. Optery now scans Google and Bing search results for your personal information. Most people don't realize that even after data brokers remove your info, search engines can still surface cached pages or aggregated results.
By monitoring search engines alongside broker sites, Optery gives you a more complete picture of your digital footprint. It's the kind of feature that separates a good privacy tool from a great one.
Free Tier Improvements

Optery's free basic plan got a meaningful upgrade. You can now access a personalized exposure report without paying a dime. No credit card required. Just sign up, run a scan, and see exactly where your data appears.

This is huge for anyone on the fence. You get real, actionable information before committing financially. The free tier includes self-service opt-out guidance too, so you can start cleaning up your digital presence immediately.
Streamlined Dashboard

The user interface received a facelift. Navigation is cleaner, status updates are clearer, and you can track removal progress across all brokers from a single view. For someone managing privacy for multiple family members, this makes a real difference.
Ready to see your exposure?
Check out Optery's latest plans and pricing
to get started.
How the Removal Process Actually Works
I get asked this a lot: does Optery actually delete your data, or does it just send emails into the void?
Here's the honest answer: it does both, and the follow-through is what impressed me.
First, Optery scans its database of brokers. This happens automatically once you're signed up. The system identifies where your information appears and generates removal requests for each site.

Then comes the submission phase. Optery handles the tedious work of contacting each broker. Some brokers make this deliberately difficult—hidden forms, confusing processes, delayed responses. Optery has figured out the optimal approach for each one.
After submission, the monitoring begins. Optery checks whether your data was actually removed. If a broker re-adds your information (which happens), Optery resubmits the request.

This ongoing monitoring is what separates Optery from DIY approaches. I've tried manually opting out of data brokers before. It took hours, and my data reappeared within months. With Optery, the process runs continuously in the background.
For more strategies on maintaining your privacy, I recommend reading our
Optery tips and best practices
Who Benefits Most from Optery
Not every tool is right for every person. Here's who I think gets the most value from Optery:
Professionals with Public Profiles

If your job involves any public visibility—sales, real estate, consulting, journalism—your information is likely scattered across dozens of broker sites. Optery helps you control that narrative without spending hours on manual requests.
People Who've Experienced Data Breaches

If your email has appeared in a data breach (check HaveIBeenPwned if you're unsure), your information is almost certainly on broker sites. Optery addresses the downstream effects of breaches that security monitoring tools miss.
Families Concerned About Safety

Parents, stalking survivors, and anyone concerned about physical safety benefit enormously. When your home address is a Google search away, that's not just annoying—it's dangerous. Optery makes that information harder to find.
Privacy-Conscious Individuals

You don't need a specific reason to want privacy. Maybe you just don't want companies profiting from your personal data. That's valid. Optery gives you a practical way to assert that preference at scale.
Business Owners and Executives

Corporate espionage and social engineering attacks often start with publicly available personal information. Removing your data from broker sites reduces your attack surface. For business leaders, this isn't paranoia—it's basic operational security.
Want to explore more scenarios? Our guide on
Optery use cases
dives deeper into specific situations.
Pricing: What You Actually Pay
Optery offers three tiers, and the free option is genuinely useful.
Free Basic
No cost, no credit card. You get a personalized exposure report with screenshots and self-service opt-out guidance. It's perfect for testing the waters and understanding your exposure level.
Core

This is where the automated removal kicks in. Optery handles the entire process for you—scanning, requesting, monitoring. The monthly price is competitive with similar services, and the yearly plan saves you about 17%.
Extended

For maximum coverage, the Extended plan includes everything in Core plus additional broker coverage, family member protection, and priority support. If you're managing privacy for multiple people, this tier makes sense.
All paid plans come with a 30-day money-back guarantee. Cancel anytime. No questions asked.
Here's my honest take: start with the free plan. Run your exposure report. If you're uncomfortable with what you see (most people are), upgrade to Core. The peace of mind alone justifies the cost.
See Optery's current pricing and sign up here
. They occasionally run promotions—I've seen discounts during holiday periods that make the annual plan especially attractive.
Why I Keep Recommending Optery
I've tested dozens of privacy tools over the years. Some are scammy. Some are well-intentioned but ineffective. A few are genuinely good.
Optery belongs in that last category, and here's why:
Transparency.
The exposure report shows you exactly what Optery found. No vague claims. Screenshots and specifics.
Automation.
Manual opt-outs are exhausting and temporary. Optery handles the ongoing work so you don't have to.
Coverage.
Hundreds of brokers, search engine monitoring, continuous updates. The scope keeps growing.
Accessibility.
A functional free tier means everyone can start protecting their privacy regardless of budget.
Results.
I've seen my own data disappear from sites where it lingered for years. That's not marketing copy—it's personal experience.
If you're still on the fence, read our deep dive on
why choose Optery
over alternatives.
The bottom line: your personal information is being bought and sold right now. Optery gives you a practical, affordable way to take it back.
Start with a free scan
and see for yourself.

children.text: Is Optery really free?

Yes, Optery offers a genuine free Basic tier that includes a personalized exposure report with screenshots and self-service opt-out guidance. No credit card is required to sign up. Paid plans unlock automated removal and ongoing monitoring across hundreds of data broker sites.
How long does it take for Optery to remove my data?

Initial removal requests are submitted within days of signing up. However, data brokers have varying response times—some process removals in a week, others take several weeks. Optery continuously monitors and resubmits requests as needed, so the process is ongoing rather than one-time.
Can data brokers re-add my information after Optery removes it?

Unfortunately, yes. Data brokers regularly scrape and update their databases, which means your information can reappear. This is exactly why Optery's ongoing monitoring feature matters—it detects reappearances and resubmits removal requests automatically, keeping your data suppressed over time.
Is Optery safe to use? Will it share my information?

Optery's business model is built on protecting your privacy, not exploiting it. They need your basic information to search for and remove your data, but they don't sell or share it with third parties. Their privacy policy clearly outlines how your data is handled and protected.
